relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job continues his discourse.
He swears by the living Divine, even while saying justice has been removed from him.
As long as breath remains in him,
his lips will not speak injustice or deceit.
He refuses to call the friends right or surrender integrity.
He holds fast to righteousness without heart-condemnation.
Let the enemy be counted with the wicked, not Job.
The godless have no secure hope when the soul is drawn out.
Will the Divine hear their cry when distress comes?
Will they delight in Shaddai and call continually?
Job says he will teach what belongs to the hand of the Divine.
The friends have seen, yet their counsel has become empty.
He names the portion of the wicked from Shaddai.
Children and bread do not secure the ruthless.
Those remaining are swallowed by loss without proper mourning.
Silver and clothing may pile up like dust and clay.
But the righteous and innocent inherit what was gathered.
The wicked house is fragile as moth-work or a watchman’s booth.
Wealth disappears when the eyes open.
Terrors and storm overtake him.
The east wind sweeps him from place.
He flees what does not spare.
His place hisses him away.
